problem: A rectangular garden 50 feet long and 10 feet wide is enclosed by a fence. To make the garden larger, while using the same fence, its shape is changed to a square. By how many square feet does this enlarge the garden?

classified_questions.question: What are the dimensions of the original rectangular garden?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: What is the area of the original rectangular garden?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the perimeter of the original rectangular garden?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How long are each side of the square garden?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the area of the square garden?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How much larger is the area of the square garden compared to the area of the original rectangular garden?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How did you find the increase in area?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can you think of any other scenarios where a similar approach can be used to find the increase in area when changing the shape of a garden or any other enclosed area?
classified_questions.label: e
